scope: Canonical model-facing web_fetch tool with cleaned rendering, domain permission rules, and preapproved-host handling.

gateSupportFiles.reason: Full verify for this item runs the TUI startup smoke because model-facing tool changes touch the TUI tool surface. The built CLI imports bundled sandbox chunks that load seatbelt policy files from runtime/dist/policies, so the build script must copy sandbox policy assets there as well as beside the launcher entrypoint.
Documents the build-asset copy contract required by the same startup gate; this is local parity documentation, not a donor citation in public commit text.

omissions.reason: AgenC runtime does not ship the Firecrawl integration or managed Firecrawl credential path; web_fetch uses the built-in HTTPS fetch/render path.
TL-08 scope is URL fetch plus cleaned rendering. AgenC returns cleaned page content and the original prompt field instead of spending a second model call inside the tool.
The donor preflight endpoint is product-specific. AgenC uses preapproved hosts plus domain-scoped permission rules for this item.
TL-08 returns cleaned text/markdown content. Binary persistence belongs to a future attachment/output-storage item.

The live AgenC path uses Node fetch, which does not expose an axios-style lookup hook. This item blocks direct private, loopback, and link-local IP literals and leaves DNS-level rebinding protection to a future HTTP-client hardening item.

requiredBehaviors: register canonical web_fetch as the visible model-facing fetch tool
keep legacy WebFetch dispatch available as a deferred compatibility entry
mark both fetch spellings as read-only shared-read tools with declared recovery categories
upgrade http URLs to https before fetching
reject non-https URLs and embedded URL credentials
validate every redirect target and final response URL before reading
reject redirects that change hostname so domain permission decisions cannot be bypassed
reject preapproved path-scoped redirects whose final URL leaves the approved path scope
read response bodies through a bounded stream before markdown conversion
convert HTML responses to markdown while removing scripts and styles
fall back to text cleanup when markdown conversion fails
truncate very large returned content with an explicit truncation marker
auto-allow hosts in the preapproved web fetch allowlist
derive content rules as domain:<hostname>
honor deny, ask, and allow rules for both web_fetch and legacy WebFetch spellings
offer localSettings allow-rule suggestions for new domain approvals
block direct private, loopback, and link-local IP literal targets before fetch
block deterministic localhost hostnames before permission approval or fetch
map plain-string web_fetch calls to the url argument field
preserve plain-string WebFetch calls for existing transcripts or deferred callers
satisfy the TL-08 registry evidence grep for web_fetch/webFetch
keep hostname-only preapproved checks O(1)
preserve path-prefix matching with path segment boundaries
treat only HTTPS URLs as preapproved when parsing full URLs
report preapproved status for the final fetched URL after redirects
use the allowed AgenC service domain for AgenC-owned preapproval entries
